page: Microsoft Azure E64s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv3 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA. It is equipped with 432.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.75 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core metrics. However, the high core count enables strong multi-threaded performance, particularly in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests, both of which rank in the top tier. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, perform in the average range. Large LLM inference tasks, such as text generation with a 70B model, are weak due to the lack of hardware acceleration, though smaller model inference remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E64s_v3 offers a dense allocation of memory relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-core CPU speeds. The inclusion of local storage and Premium Storage support adds value for data-heavy operations. This server type is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, multi-threaded data processing, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, a 2.6 GHz clock speed, and 432.0 GB of RAM. The instance includes 885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support and no GPU. Benchmarks show weak single-core performance but strong multi-threaded capabilities, particularly in multi-threaded decompression and extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis throughput remain average. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density, making it cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that benefit from high core counts rather than peak single-core speed. It is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 64 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage. It offers strong multi-threaded performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB storage, built for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs and local storage for demanding database and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
432.0 GB of system memory providing 6.75 GB of RAM per vCPU
885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded performance in decompression and extended instructions
Well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
